feedback.detail_placeholder_specific: Describe the specific issue, e.g. what the correct data should be…
feedback.thanks_body: We've received your feedback and will work on improvements promptly.
feedback.disabled_desc: The admin has temporarily disabled feedback submission, please try again later.
feedback.err_no_type: Please select at least one issue type

docs.title: API Documentation
docs.description: Next Whois provides a simple REST API for programmatic WHOIS/RDAP lookups and dynamic OG image generation. All endpoints are publicly accessible and require no authentication.
docs.lookup_description: Query WHOIS/RDAP information for a domain, IP address, ASN, or CIDR range. RDAP and WHOIS are queried in parallel; structured fields prefer RDAP data, falling back to WHOIS. Both raw responses are included.
docs.lookup_query_desc: Domain name, IPv4/IPv6 address, ASN (e.g. AS13335), or CIDR range. Alias: q
docs.og_description: Generate a dynamic Open Graph image with WHOIS details. Internally queries /api/lookup and renders a card-style summary. Returns a PNG image.
docs.og_query_desc: Domain name, IP, ASN, or CIDR to look up and display. Alias: q
docs.rate_limiting_desc1: Successful WHOIS lookup responses are cached server-side (Redis) and served with
docs.rate_limiting_time: in the JSON body and report
docs.api_key_desc: When the administrator enables API Key verification, all public APIs (WHOIS, DNS, SSL, IP lookup) require a valid API Key in the request, otherwise HTTP 401 is returned.
docs.api_key_scope_api: WHOIS/RDAP, DNS records, SSL certificate, IP/ASN lookup
docs.api_key_403: Key invalid, disabled, expired or insufficient permissions
docs.whois_desc: Unified lookup endpoint supporting domain WHOIS/RDAP, IPv4, IPv6, ASN (autonomous system), and CIDR subnet — auto-detects input format and routes to the appropriate protocol.
docs.whois_query_desc: Query: domain / IPv4 / IPv6 / ASN (e.g. AS15169) / CIDR (e.g. 1.1.1.0/24)
docs.whois_note1: Domain lookup prefers RDAP protocol, falls back to WHOIS on failure; the source field indicates the protocol used
docs.whois_note2: Cache hit: cached: true, time: 0; cache TTL: s-maxage=3600
docs.whois_note3: IP / ASN / CIDR queries are routed via IANA RDAP bootstrap to the corresponding regional registry (ARIN / RIPE / APNIC etc.)
docs.whois_note4: Rate limiting: max 40 requests per IP per minute; exceeding returns HTTP 429
docs.dns_records_desc: Query any DNS records in parallel via four DoH resolvers (Google, Cloudflare, Quad9, AdGuard), returning deduplicated merged results and per-resolver latency.
docs.dns_records_name_desc: Domain to query, e.g. google.com or _dmarc.google.com
docs.dns_records_type_desc: Record type: A · AAAA · MX · NS · CNAME · TXT · SOA · CAA · SRV
docs.dns_records_note1: No caching (Cache-Control: no-store), data fetched in real time
docs.dns_records_note2: TXT records return raw values; parse SPF / DMARC / DKIM / BIMI yourself
docs.dns_records_note3: Record not found: found: false, flat: []
docs.dns_records_note4: Single resolver timeout: 7s, four run in parallel, overall usually completes in 1–2s
docs.dns_txt_desc: Dedicated TXT record query, using four standard DNS resolvers (Google, Cloudflare, Quad9, OpenDNS) in parallel — ideal for verifying SPF, DMARC, DKIM and other email security configurations.
docs.dns_txt_name_desc: Domain or subdomain to query, e.g. google.com or _dmarc.example.com
docs.dns_txt_diff_use2: General DNS record queries, supports more types
docs.ssl_desc: Connects directly to the target host (default port 443), performs a TLS handshake and returns SSL certificate details: validity period, issuer, SAN list, certificate chain, cipher suites, and days remaining.
docs.ssl_hostname_desc: Target hostname or IP, e.g. google.com, github.com or 1.1.1.1
docs.ssl_port_desc: Target TLS port (default 443, SMTPS uses 465, IMAPS uses 993, etc.)
docs.ssl_note1: Certificate chain includes complete intermediate CA info from server to root CA
docs.ssl_note2: Connection timeout: 10s; unreachable hosts return ok: false + error field
docs.ip_desc: Queries IP geolocation, ASN, ISP, organization, and routing information for IPv4/IPv6 addresses or ASN numbers.
docs.ip_input_desc: IPv4 address, IPv6 address, or ASN number (e.g. 8.8.8.8, 2001:db8::1, AS15169)
docs.ip_note1: Geolocation data from MaxMind GeoLite2, ASN data from routeviews
docs.ip_note2: ASN query returns all prefixes announced by the AS
docs.ip_note3: Private IP addresses and reserved ranges return limited information
docs.tools_desc: Generates an Open Graph preview image (og:image) for a given domain, with customizable width, height, and theme.
docs.icp_desc: Query ICP filing records for Chinese mainland websites and apps. Supports domain lookup, app/mini-program filing, and blacklist queries.
docs.icp_query_value_desc: Domain name or ICP number to query
docs.icp_note1: ICP data from MIIT (Ministry of Industry and Information Technology) official API
docs.icp_note2: Blacklist queries (types starting with b) require special permissions
docs.icp_note3: Results are paginated; use the page parameter to fetch additional pages
docs.icp_fields_serviceName: Service name (APP, mini-program, or quick app name)
docs.icp_fields_blackListLevel: Threat level (only for violation types; value 2 = no current violation)
docs.icp_rate_note: This API requires an API Key with api or all scope
docs.icp_disclaimer: Data provided for reference only; official MIIT website prevails
docs.rate_limit_note: When /api/lookup hits cache: cached: true and time: 0. Exceeding limit returns HTTP 429 with X-RateLimit-Limit / Remaining / Reset headers.

validation.empty: Type a domain, IP address, or ASN to get started
validation.no_dot: Domains need a dot — try something like example.com
validation.too_long: That's way too long — WHOIS servers won't handle more than 253 characters
validation.label_too_long: Each domain segment can be at most 63 characters
validation.invalid_chars: Odd characters detected — stick to letters, digits, and hyphens
validation.invalid_domain: Invalid format — labels can't start or end with a hyphen
validation.invalid_tld: '{{tld}}' is too short to be a real TLD — must be at least 2 letters
validation.unknown_tld: '{{tld}}' isn't in the ICANN TLD registry — typo? (try .com, .net, .io)

stamp.title: Brand Claim
stamp.subtitle: Verify DNS ownership to add a trusted brand tag
stamp.how_step1_desc: Choose a tag style and enter a name and contact info. The tag will appear in everyone's lookup results.
stamp.how_step2_desc: Add one TXT record in your DNS console to prove you manage this domain.
stamp.how_step3_desc: The system checks your DNS record automatically. Once verified, the tag is live globally — no manual review.
stamp.domain_hint: You must have DNS management access
stamp.desc_placeholder: Briefly describe what this tag is for...
stamp.email_hint: Used to receive verification results, not shown publicly
stamp.err_submit_failed: Submission failed, please check your network
stamp.verify_dns_desc: Log in to your DNS console and add the record below. The system checks every {{sec}}s.
stamp.fail_msg: TXT record not found. Please confirm it was added correctly — DNS propagation can take minutes to hours.
stamp.dns_error_msg: DNS query failed. Please confirm the domain is valid and try again.
stamp.done_delete_body: Verification is complete — the TXT record is no longer needed. You can delete it from your DNS console to keep things clean:
stamp.done_keep_record: Keeping the record won't affect tag display — deleting is optional.
stamp.done_claim_body: Brand claim is live. Lookups for {{domain}} will now show:
stamp.done_next_all_users: All users who look up this domain will see your tag
stamp.done_next_prominent: The tag is displayed prominently at the top of the domain detail page
stamp.done_next_resubmit: You can resubmit at any time to update the tag
stamp.dns_prop_suffix: to propagate globally. Closing this page won't lose your progress.
stamp.page_desc_main: Claim your domain and display your brand info in WHOIS lookup results.
stamp.empty_claims_hint: Search and claim your domain to display your brand in WHOIS results
stamp.pending_status: Pending · Click Continue to complete DNS verification
stamp.login_manage_desc: Log in to view all your claims and manage verification status
stamp.desktop_hint: Click Brand Claim at the top of domain results
stamp.search_title: Enter a domain to start claiming
stamp.search_redirect_hint: You will be taken to the brand claim flow for this domain
stamp.landing_step1_desc: Type the domain you own in the homepage search box or the input below
stamp.landing_step2_desc: Find the purple shield button at the top of the domain WHOIS detail page and click it
stamp.landing_step3_desc: Add a TXT record or upload a verification file to prove you own the domain
stamp.landing_step4_desc: After verification, WHOIS lookups will show your brand name and a certified tag
stamp.info_note: Brand claims require DNS TXT record or file-path verification to confirm domain ownership. Verification usually completes within minutes.
stamp.link_upgrade_hint: Upgrade to set a personal or brand website link
stamp.desc_upgrade_hint: Upgrade to add a brand or personal description (max 300 characters)
stamp.member_title_hint: Members can set up to 20-character tags
stamp.vercel_verify_desc: Confirm domain ownership by adding Vercel official TXT record to DNS.
stamp.cannot_get_vercel: Could not get Vercel verification record
stamp.add_txt_record: Add this TXT record in your DNS panel
stamp.record_note: Record type: TXT. Propagation time varies by provider, usually within minutes
stamp.click_verify_after: Click verify once the record is added
stamp.token_mismatch: TXT record found but token does not match
stamp.token_mismatch_note: Please delete the old record and re-add the correct Token value shown above, then wait for DNS propagation and retry.
stamp.check_content_note: DNS propagated to the verification node, but the Token value does not match. Please check the record was copied completely.
stamp.auto_checked_n: Auto-checked {{n}} times, no TXT record detected
stamp.tld_no_dns_note: Your TLD may not support standard DNS TXT queries. We recommend switching to file verification, which completes in seconds without waiting for DNS propagation.
stamp.add_txt_first: Please add the TXT record as described above, then click Verify Now or wait for auto-check
stamp.host_note: Only enter the prefix for the host field; your DNS panel will append the domain suffix automatically. Do not enter the full domain.
stamp.file_verify_desc: Place a file on your website's root. Verification completes in seconds — no DNS propagation needed.
stamp.file_verify_step2: Confirm the file is publicly accessible
stamp.record_note_txt: Record type: TXT. Propagation time varies by provider, usually within minutes.
stamp.host_prefix_note: Only enter the prefix — your DNS panel will append the domain suffix automatically.
stamp.no_txt_at_host: No TXT records found at this hostname — verify the record was added, then try again

remind.desktop_hint: ↑ Click “Domain Subscription” at the top of results
remind.first_time_desc: View the guide to learn how to look up domains and subscribe to expiry reminders.

remind.need_invite_desc: Domain subscription requires an invitation code. Contact admin to get one.

guide.page_meta_desc: Learn how to look up domain WHOIS information and subscribe to expiry reminders to protect your domain assets.
guide.header_subtitle: 3 steps to enable domain expiry reminders
guide.hero_desc: Look up domain WHOIS info and subscribe to expiry reminder emails. Setup takes just 3 steps.
guide.cta_hint: After searching, click the 🔔 icon on the domain card to subscribe
guide.step1_subtitle: Look up any domain on the home page
guide.step1_desc: Go to the home page, type the domain you want to monitor (e.g. google.com) in the search box, then click Search.
guide.step1_tip: Supports internationalized domains (IDN) and all TLDs such as .com, .io, etc.
guide.step2_subtitle: Set a reminder in one click
guide.step2_desc: On the result page, click the 🔔 bell icon next to the domain card to open the reminder subscription page.
guide.step2_tip: Can't find the icon? Click the top-right area of the domain card.
guide.step3_subtitle: Configure how and when to be notified
guide.step3_desc: Enter the email address for notifications, choose how many days in advance (e.g. 60, 30, 10 days), then click 'Enable Expiry Reminder'.
guide.step3_tip: After logging in you can manage or cancel subscriptions anytime in the dashboard.
guide.feature2: Grace period · Redemption period · Pending delete — full monitoring
guide.feature3: Registrar, DNS, DNSSEC key info at a glance
